verbatim_text: You feed this image to your network, and it says the wrong category. Okay, what can I do? Well, in deep learning, the newer networks, you really learn intermediate feature representations of this image in order to arrive at the final answer. And, you know, let's say that everything up to, you know, the end of the rest stuff is your feature representation. So the wrong prediction really happens, you know, often because you have bad features. The idea of self-supervised learning is to improve the features with only unlabeled input. And let me make some space here and show you an example that's called mass reconstruction. So I'm going to apply a random mask to this image over here and feed it to this network. And in order to do reconstruction, I have to give it a reconstruction head. It's a different head from the yellow thing, right? And the reconstruction head would output a reconstruction of the original image. And the goal of training for this self-supervised learning task is to better reconstruct the original image. So you compare them. They don't quite look the same.
